reasoning: The question defines invasion strictly as a 'military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran'. An invasion of this scale against a large, populous, and geographically defensible country like Iran would require an enormous military buildup taking many months, analogous to the lead-up to the 2003 Iraq War. It is extremely rare for the US to launch ground invasions to hold territory, and doing so against Iran has been consistently avoided by successive administrations due to the massive costs and risks. Furthermore, the knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, leaving only 52 days for the event to happen before the December 31 deadline. Without prior knowledge of a massive, unprecedented ongoing deployment, the probability of an invasion occurring in this short remaining window is near zero. I am setting the probability at the minimum allowable boundary (0.02).
